title: wasm storage::load_identity returns plain [u8;64]; key bytes not zeroized
description: wasm/src/storage.rs::load_identity() returns a plain [u8; 64]. In api.rs::load_identity (line 74) the Ok(arr) binding holds 64 bytes of key material on the Wasm linear memory heap until it is dropped without zeroing. Additionally, intermediate String values (json_str, secret_b64) holding base64 of the key remain alive until GC. In the Wasm linear memory model there is no OS-level protection; a same-origin JS caller with access to the Wasm memory buffer can read these values. Same class as kef6.36 but distinct codepath (storage.rs, not keyfile.rs).
